mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real auto sales and low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer demand supported by cheap credit, creating a positive feedback loop that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns, or the signal information coefficient falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 190, persistent inflation forces monetary tightening expectations that compress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 190, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for lagged rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts combined with low unemployment signal strong household balance sheets and construction sector momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant after adjusting for macro volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real gross domestic product reflects contracting aggregate demand and falling corporate profits, which directly causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during downturn phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during contraction periods, or the predictive relationship reverses sign in out-of-sample validation.
